description: AirTrack Logbook application sitemap. Use this to answer navigation questions like 'where do I add an aircraft' or 'how do I edit a flight'. Always include the url in your answer as a clickable link.

pages.name: Home / Dashboard
pages.description: The main dashboard showing summary statistics: total aircraft logged, total flights, total airlines, and recent activity.

pages.name: Aircraft List
pages.description: Browse and search all aircraft in the logbook. Shows registration, type, airline, and photo thumbnail. Supports filtering and sorting.

pages.name: Add Aircraft
pages.description: Add a new aircraft to the logbook. Enter registration, aircraft type, airline, MSN, flight number, date spotted, airport, and notes. This is where you log a new aircraft sighting.

pages.name: Edit Aircraft
pages.description: Edit an existing aircraft record. Access by clicking the edit button on any aircraft in the aircraft list or aircraft detail page.

pages.name: Aircraft Detail / Info
pages.description: Full detail page for a single aircraft: all logged fields, photo gallery, ownership history, and cross-referenced registry data.

pages.name: Upload Aircraft Image
pages.description: Upload a photo for an aircraft in the logbook. Supports JPEG and PNG. Images are linked to the aircraft record by registration.

pages.name: Airlines List
pages.description: Browse all airlines in the database. Shows airline name, ICAO/IATA codes, country, and logo.

pages.name: Add Airline
pages.description: Add a new airline to the database. Enter name, ICAO code, IATA code, country, and upload a logo.

pages.name: Airline Detail / Info
pages.description: Full detail page for a single airline: all fields, logo, and list of aircraft spotted from that airline.

pages.name: Reports
pages.description: Pre-built reports and the Smart Ask AI query box. Reports include: most seen aircraft, top airlines, busiest airports, oldest aircraft, rare airline sightings, different aircraft types, orphaned aircraft. Smart Ask lets you ask natural language questions about your logbook data.

pages.name: Flight History
pages.description: Chronological sighting history for a specific aircraft. Access from the aircraft detail page.

pages.name: Logged Airports
pages.description: List of all airports where aircraft have been spotted, with sighting counts.

pages.name: Orphaned Aircraft
pages.description: Aircraft records that are missing an airline link or have other data quality issues.

pages.name: Live Flights
pages.description: External link to Flightradar24 for real-time flight tracking. Opens in a new tab.

pages.name: User Guide
pages.description: Built-in help and documentation for AirTrack. Covers all features including adding aircraft, airlines, images, and using reports.

pages.name: Cockpit / Admin
pages.description: Admin dashboard for server management. Includes database status, logs, broken link checker, git controls, and system settings. Also called the Cockpit.

pages.name: Admin Logs
pages.description: View application logs. Shows recent activity, errors, and system events.

pages.name: Admin Status
pages.description: System status page showing database connection health, disk usage, and application version.

pages.name: Broken Links
pages.description: Scan for aircraft records with missing or broken image links.

pages.name: Manual Aircraft Entry
pages.description: Alternative aircraft entry form for manual data entry without registry lookups.

pages.name: Link Airline Logos
pages.description: Tool to batch-link airline logo images to airline records.
